title: Pilot deployment and tenant onboarding
description: Turn the pilot-ready product into something that can be stood up repeatedly for a real tenant without code surgery. Add a bounded tenant onboarding path, deployment checklist surface, and configuration bootstrap so a new customer environment can be initialized in a disciplined way.
implementation_tasks: define the minimum tenant bootstrap contract for owners, repos, delivery destinations, policies, and enabled packs
add a deployment or onboarding status surface so operators can see whether the environment is ready for use
persist bootstrap completeness and configuration health in a product-readable way
document the exact onboarding steps for a new tenant and make the UI reflect missing setup clearly
done_when: a new tenant can be configured through a bounded onboarding workflow instead of ad hoc code edits
the product shows onboarding or deployment readiness clearly before operators start triaging incidents
tenant bootstrap gaps are visible and actionable instead of surfacing only as downstream runtime errors

title: Auth, roles, and approval controls hardening
description: Harden the product’s approval and operator-control surface so the market-ready workflow has believable role separation. Replay, downstream handoff, admin controls, and Guardian approval actions should be gated by clearer roles and more explicit authorization rules.
implementation_tasks: define the bounded role matrix for operator, reviewer, and admin capabilities
apply role gating consistently to replay, handoff send, settings, and approval actions
surface authorization posture in the UI so unavailable controls read as policy-driven rather than broken
document the role model and control boundaries for real pilot or early production usage
done_when: critical actions are gated by a consistent role model
the UI makes role-based unavailability explicit and understandable
approval and administrative actions feel governed instead of demo-open

title: Integration reliability and delivery guarantees
description: Move downstream handoff integrations from first working state toward reliable workflow plumbing. Delivery attempts, retries, failures, and operator recovery should be visible and auditable so GitHub or Slack handoffs feel trustworthy enough for repeat use.
implementation_tasks: define delivery-attempt lifecycle states for downstream sends
persist retryable failure versus terminal failure semantics
add operator-visible recovery or retry affordances where appropriate
ensure delivery audit trails remain aligned with evidence posture and case history
done_when: downstream handoff actions expose delivery lifecycle and failure state clearly
operators can tell whether a send succeeded, needs retry, or failed terminally
integration behavior feels durable enough for repeated workflow use

title: Product observability and self-monitoring
description: Make NEXUS observable as a product, not only as a demo. Operators and maintainers should be able to see health, latency, replay failure rates, integration failures, and product workflow bottlenecks so the system itself can be trusted in real usage.
implementation_tasks: define a bounded product-observability model for request health, replay health, and downstream integration health
surface key health and latency summaries in a product-facing status area
persist or expose operational anomalies that matter for trust in the product itself
keep observability safe and concise without leaking sensitive evidence
done_when: the product exposes its own health in a way operators can understand
replay and downstream handoff failures are visible in self-monitoring surfaces
the product no longer feels like a black box when its own workflow degrades

title: Security and secrets handling hardening
description: Harden the early production posture around secrets, sensitive evidence, and downstream integration credentials. The goal is not full enterprise compliance, but a credible market-ready v1 baseline that avoids obvious trust blockers.
implementation_tasks: audit where secrets and integration credentials enter the product
tighten masking, storage, and display behavior for sensitive configuration and evidence
ensure downstream integrations and approval workflows do not leak secrets or unsafe payloads into user-facing surfaces
document the security posture and known boundaries honestly
done_when: sensitive configuration and secrets are handled more safely across product surfaces
credential-bearing integrations do not expose unsafe data in ordinary operator flows
the product has an honest documented security baseline for market-ready v1

title: Operator onboarding and runbooks
description: Reduce founder dependence by producing operator-ready onboarding flows and product runbooks. A new operator should be able to understand how to use NEXUS for triage, replay, handoff, review, and feedback without relying on internal context.
implementation_tasks: define the core operator journeys that need onboarding support
add in-product or adjacent docs for triage, replay, handoff, approval, and feedback usage
tighten wording across surfaces so the workflow is understandable without sales narration
document known boundaries, escalation paths, and recovery steps for common operator mistakes
done_when: a new operator can understand the primary workflow without direct founder guidance
the product has usable runbooks for the main market-ready v1 flows
operator-facing documentation matches the shipped workflow and its boundaries

title: Buyer-facing proof package and ROI story
description: Turn the product’s operational improvements into a buyer-ready proof package. The goal is to make the ROI story, flagship incident case studies, and measured-versus-estimated gains easy to present without hand-built founder slides every time.
implementation_tasks: define the buyer-proof artifacts for market-ready v1
turn existing ROI and case-history signals into presentation-ready product outputs or docs
prepare before-and-after workflow evidence for the flagship outage classes
keep all claims aligned with what the product actually measures today
done_when: the product and docs together provide a coherent buyer-facing proof package
ROI, relay reduction, and workflow acceleration claims are tied to visible evidence
market-facing materials remain aligned with the bounded product reality

title: Market-facing wow-effect UI revamp and conversion polish
description: Give NEXUS a clearly more premium, memorable, customer-attracting presentation layer without breaking the support-triage truthfulness of the product. This is not a random redesign; it is a deliberate visual and information-design pass so the product creates a stronger first impression for buyers, operators, and demo viewers.
implementation_tasks: define a stronger market-facing visual direction for the core product surfaces while preserving the established product narrative
upgrade the most buyer-visible screens with more distinctive typography, spacing, hierarchy, motion, and presentation polish
make the flagship incident flow feel premium and legible without hiding evidence quality or bounded-product limitations
ensure the wow-effect polish still supports usability, responsiveness, and truthful workflow comprehension
done_when: the product feels materially more premium and memorable on first impression
the flagship queue, incident, training, and handoff surfaces present a stronger buyer-facing visual identity
the UI polish improves clarity and attraction without regressing product truthfulness or workflow legibility

title: Market-ready v1 checkpoint and release readiness
description: Close the market-ready hardening phase with a deliberate release-readiness checkpoint. Confirm the bounded support-triage product is truthful, operable, buyer-explainable, and deployment-ready for a narrow v1 without drifting into unsupported claims.
implementation_tasks: run a full validation pass across backend tests, browser flows, demo flow, and deployment path
refresh owner-facing docs so the market-ready story, boundaries, and deployment posture are current
write a release-readiness summary that states what is real, what is bounded, and what remains out of scope
treat this item as the explicit gate before any new breadth-expansion backlog is written
done_when: the repo ends the phase with a clear release-readiness checkpoint
owner-facing docs clearly state the market-ready v1 scope and boundaries
the product can be described as a narrow sellable support-triage v1 without hand-waving
